meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Solve Debugging Issues for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to ship professional, production-ready apps faster as a freelancer.
content.answer_block: When debugging feels like a bottomless pit, stop writing from scratch. Use a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to bake in security, auth, and database architecture from day one. By pairing this with AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate error resolution and ship features significantly faster.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve been staring at a stack trace for three hours, and the client’s deadline is breathing down your neck. For the modern freelancer, the gap between 'idea' and 'production' isn't just code—it’s the mounting technical debt that turns every minor update into a debugging nightmare. You aren't just coding; you're firefighting.

But there is a better way. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you shift from 'manual laborer' to 'architect.' You stop debugging boilerplate and start building value. With the right AI-assisted workflow, you can bypass the grit and move straight to deployment, ensuring your freelance business stays profitable, scalable, and—most importantly—stress-free.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Production-Ready Starter
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested SaaS boilerplate to ensure your architecture is solid before you write your first custom line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project architecture that includes Stripe integration, Next.js, and Supabase auth. Ensure the folder structure follows industry-standard patterns for scalability.

steps.title: Configure Automated Error Catching
steps.description: Set up global error boundaries within your starter kit so that unhandled exceptions are caught and logged before they crash the user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a global error boundary and a custom hook for capturing frontend runtime errors. Output the code in a reusable utility file.

steps.title: Audit Existing Logic for Anti-Patterns
steps.description: Use AI to scan your current codebase for common bugs and inefficient patterns that are likely causing your current debugging headaches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the current project for potential memory leaks, race conditions, and inefficient database queries. Prioritize fixes by impact.

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Generate comprehensive test suites for your core business logic to catch bugs during development rather than during client review.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest unit tests for all services in the /api folder. Ensure 90% coverage for happy and edge cases.

steps.title: Deploy with Continuous Integration
steps.description: Ensure your production environment is stable by automating your build and deployment pipeline, preventing 'it works on my machine' scenarios.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and a build check before any merge to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always treat your starter kit as a read-only template; create a local clone or fork so you can pull upstream updates without breaking your custom logic.
Use Claude Code to document your components as you build them; future-you will save hours when you return to a project after a month.
Leverage Antigravity to generate 'mock data factories' for your database, which makes testing complex edge cases trivial.
When facing a cryptic bug, feed the entire stack trace and the relevant file contents into Claude Code; it’s significantly more accurate than standard LLM chat windows.
Don't optimize early; use the starter kit's default styles and database schema until you hit a performance bottleneck.
content.next_steps: After completing your setup, perform a dry run of your deployment process and schedule a 30-minute stress-test session to verify that your automated error handlers trigger correctly.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start scaling your freelance business with a system that works for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
